Title: Flavin-containing monooxygenases in plants: looking beyond detox

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for basal defence and systemic acquired resistance, associated with salicylic acid accumulation in systemic tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: activation tagging; stable overexpression; knockout mutant analysis; pathogen infection assays; transcript accumulation analysis; mutagenesis of FAD/NADPH-binding motifs
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly induced by pathogen infection; >17-fold upregulated in acd11; elevated in lsd1; induced by superoxide; accumulates in local and systemic leaves during SAR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FMO1 overexpression enhances resistance to virulent and avirulent bacterial and oomycete pathogens, whereas fmo1 mutants show enhanced susceptibility. 2. FMO1 is required for EDS1/PAD4-dependent basal and TIR-NB-LRR-mediated defence, but not CC-NB-LRR-mediated defence. 3. fmo1 mutants fail to establish systemic acquired resistance and lack systemic salicylic acid accumulation and PR gene induction after local infection.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: salicylic acid and SAR are central to pathogen defence
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering broad-spectrum disease resistance and SAR competence, but biochemical substrate and possible redox side effects remain unclear.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pathogen infection; local infection triggering SAR; superoxide-related stress
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: activation tagging; stable overexpression (promoter not specified)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: local infected leaves and untreated systemic leaves during SAR
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This review emphasizes defence role, but the direct metabolic substrate/product of FMO1 remains unknown; may act via redox regulation, detoxification, glutathione oxidation or ROS-related mechanisms rather than a defined biosynthetic step.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FMO family; Rossmann-fold FAD-binding motif; NADPH-binding motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: probably catalyzes conversion of tryptamine to N-hydroxyl tryptamine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies immediate biosynthetic intermediate for auxin production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a probable rate-limiting step in auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression increases free auxin levels and causes auxin overproduction phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: activation tagging; overexpression; recombinant protein assay in vitro; iaaL suppression of phenotype; metabolite measurement
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Activation-tagged yucca mutants overexpress YUC1 and show elongated hypocotyls, epinastic cotyledons, elongated petioles, shorter roots and more abundant root hairs. 2. Free auxin levels are increased by at least 50% in yucca mutants. 3. Recombinant YUCCA protein converts tryptamine to N-hydroxyl tryptamine, supporting a direct biosynthetic role.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to modulate endogenous auxin biosynthesis, but constitutive overexpression causes strong pleiotropic developmental defects.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: auxin overproduction causes severe abnormal morphology and root/shoot architecture changes
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: activation tagging; constitutive overexpression (specific promoter not stated)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes prior studies; functional redundancy exists within the YUCCA family.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: inferred YUCCA-family auxin biosynthetic monooxygenase activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression causes auxin overproduction phenotypes; loss together with YUC6 contributes to developmental defects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; double/triple/quadruple mutant analysis; iaaM rescue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: partially overlapping temporal and spatial expression in shoot apices and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of YUCCA2 causes phenotypes similar to YUC1 overexpression. 2. Single mutants show no visible phenotype, but higher-order mutants reveal redundancy. 3. Together with YUC4 and YUC6, YUC2 contributes to auxin-dependent development.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter plant architecture through auxin biosynthesis, but redundancy and pleiotropy must be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: developmental defects upon perturbation of auxin homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred within redundant YUCCA family.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: inferred YUCCA-family auxin biosynthetic monooxygenase activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUCCA3 overexpression produces phenotypes similar to YUC1 overexpression. 2. This supports functional redundancy among YUCCA family members in auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter auxin levels, with caution due to pleiotropic morphology defects.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a close homolog with redundant function.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: inferred YUCCA-family auxin biosynthetic monooxygenase activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression causes elevated-auxin-associated morphology; combined loss with other YUC genes causes severe developmental defects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; double/triple/quadruple mutant analysis; iaaM rescue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: partially overlapping temporal and spatial expression in shoot apices and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUC4 is redundantly required for normal floral patterning and vascular formation. 2. Overexpression of YUC4/THREAD causes phenotypes associated with elevated auxin levels. 3. Higher-order mutants demonstrate that YUC4 contributes to auxin biosynthesis-dependent development.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering plant architecture, but requires careful dosage control due to redundancy and pleiotropy.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: developmental abnormalities when auxin homeostasis is perturbed

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: inferred YUCCA-family auxin biosynthetic monooxygenase activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression elevates auxin and suppresses weak erecta inflorescence defects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: activation tagging/overexpression; genetic suppression; auxin measurement
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Activation-tagged super1-D overexpresses YUCCA5. 2. Elevated auxin levels in super1-D restore reduced peduncle and pedicel elongation and plant height of weak erecta mutant er-103. 3. Findings are consistent with a role in regulating auxin levels.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be exploited to modify inflorescence architecture through auxin modulation, but likely causes pleiotropic growth effects.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: altered architecture due to excess auxin
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also known as SUPER1; identified as suppressor of erecta phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: inferred YUCCA-family auxin biosynthetic monooxygenase activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: combined loss with other YUC genes causes increasingly severe developmental defects
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: partially overlapping temporal and spatial expression in shoot apices and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUC6 acts redundantly with YUC2 and YUC4. 2. Single mutants lack obvious phenotype, but higher-order mutants display floral and vascular defects. 3. iaaM-mediated auxin production rescues triple mutant defects, supporting YUC6 participation in auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for multiplex engineering of auxin biosynthesis, but redundancy requires multigene strategies and raises pleiotropy risk.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: severe developmental defects in higher-order mutants disrupting auxin homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional redundancy is a key feature.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: inferred YUCCA-like auxin biosynthetic monooxygenase activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of function alters leaf and flower architecture; overexpression causes YUCCA-like auxin overproduction phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLOOZY is a petunia orthologue of Arabidopsis YUCCA genes. 2. Loss of function alters leaf and flower architecture. 3. Overexpression causes phenotypes similar to YUCCA overexpression, supporting a conserved role in auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests conserved utility of YUCCA-like FMOs for architecture engineering in dicots, with pleiotropy concerns.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: abnormal leaf and flower architecture upon misexpression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence presented as conservation of YUCCA function in petunia.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: inferred YUCCA-like auxin biosynthetic monooxygenase activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression increases IAA and causes auxin overproduction phenotypes; antisense expression causes defects similar to auxin-insensitive mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsYUCCA1 overexpression increases IAA levels and produces characteristic auxin overproduction phenotypes. 2. Antisense OsYUCCA1 causes developmental defects resembling rice auxin-insensitive mutants. 3. Supports conservation of YUCCA function in monocot auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for modifying rice architecture through auxin biosynthesis, but misexpression likely causes strong developmental penalties.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: developmental defects from altered auxin homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: constitutive overexpression; antisense expression (promoters not specified)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Seven rice YUCCA genes were cloned, but OsYUCCA1 was functionally characterized in detail.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FMO family; clade III S-oxygenating FMO
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts methylthioalkyl glucosinolates into methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates by S-oxygenation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as rate limiting in wild-type plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression causes a fivefold increase in methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; recombinant protein assay; overexpression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FMOGS-OX was identified by co-regulation with known glucosinolate pathway genes. 2. Recombinant FMOGS-OX converts methylthioalkyl glucosinolates to methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates. 3. Overexpression causes a fivefold increase in methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates, indicating rate-limiting activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to elevate health-related glucosinolate-derived isothiocyanate potential in Brassicaceae crops.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Enzyme is selective for methylthioalkyl glucosinolates; corresponding precursors lacking the S-glucose group are not substrates.
